title: NDBC Salinity Map
text:
Code
library(dplyr)
library(httr2)
library(htmltools)
library(knitr)
library(leaflet)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(viridisLite)

# Station list and parameters mirror ndbc_fetch_airflow_dag.py
inwater_params <- c("sea_water_practical_salinity", "sea_water_temperature")

ndbc_stations <- list(
  list(name = "bkyf1", long_name = "Buoy_Key"),
  list(name = "bnkf1", long_name = "Butternut_Kay"),
  list(name = "bobf1", long_name = "Bob_Allen_Key"),
  list(name = "bwsf1", long_name = "Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "dkkf1", long_name = "Duck_Key"),
  list(name = "gbtf1", long_name = "Garfield_Bight"),
  list(name = "hcef1", long_name = "Highway_Creek"),
  list(name = "jbyf1", long_name = "Joe_Bay"),
  list(name = "jkyf1", long_name = "Johnson_Key"),
  list(name = "lbsf1", long_name = "Little_Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "lmdf1", long_name = "Little_Madeira_Bay"),
  list(name = "lrkf1", long_name = "Little_Rabbit_Key"),
  list(name = "lsnf1", long_name = "Long_Sound"),
  list(name = "mdkf1", long_name = "Middle_Key"),
  list(name = "mukf1", long_name = "Murray_Key"),
  list(name = "pkyf1", long_name = "Peterson_Key"),
  list(name = "tbyf1", long_name = "Terrapin_Bay"),
  list(name = "thrf1", long_name = "Thursday_Point"),
  list(name = "wrbf1", long_name = "Whipray_Basin")
)

# 25-hour lookback window
end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")
start_time <- format(Sys.time() - 25 * 3600, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")

fetch_latest_salinity <- function(station_info) {
  param <- "sea_water_practical_salinity"
  url <- sprintf(
    "https://erddap.secoora.org/erddap/tabledap/gov-nps-ever-%s.csv?time%%2Clatitude%%2Clongitude%%2C%s&time%%3E=%s&time%%3C=%s",
    station_info$name,
    param,
    start_time,
    end_time
  )

  resp <- request(url) |>
    req_perform()

  df <- read_csv(resp_body_string(resp), show_col_types = FALSE)
  names(df) <- c("time", "latitude", "longitude", "salinity")
  df <- df[-1, ] |>
    mutate(
      time = as.POSIXct(time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC"),
      latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
      longitude = as.numeric(longitude),
      salinity = as.numeric(salinity)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(salinity))

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  latest <- df[which.max(df$time), ]
  tibble(
    station_id = station_info$name,
    station = station_info$long_name,
    location = sprintf(
      "%s (%s)",
      station_info$long_name,
      sub("f1$", "", station_info$name)
    ),
    time = latest$time,
    latitude = latest$latitude,
    longitude = latest$longitude,
    salinity = latest$salinity
  )
}

salinity_data <- bind_rows(lapply(ndbc_stations, fetch_latest_salinity))
report_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")
Data are pulled from SECOORA ERDDAP: using a 25-hour window ending at render time, with the most recent non-missing salinity value kept for each station.
Report generated: 2026-07-14 10:51 EDT
Query window: 2026-07-13T13:50:52Z to 2026-07-14T14:50:52Z
Code
if (nrow(salinity_data) == 0) {
  stop("No salinity data returned from ERDDAP for the current time window.")
}

salinity_data |>
  arrange(desc(time)) |>
  mutate(
    time = format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC"),
    salinity = number(salinity, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) |>
  select(Station = station, `Station ID` = station_id, Time = time, `Salinity (PSU)` = salinity) |>
  kable()

Code
salinity_palette <- colorNumeric(
  palette = c("#2166ac", "#67a9cf", "#d1e5f0", "#fddbc7", "#ef8a62", "#b2182b"),
  domain = salinity_data$salinity,
  na.color = "#999999"
)

leaflet(salinity_data) |>
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") |>
  setView(
    lng = mean(salinity_data$longitude),
    lat = mean(salinity_data$latitude),
    zoom = 9
  ) |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius = 8,
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = 1,
    color = "#333333",
    fillColor = ~salinity_palette(salinity),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    label = ~sprintf(
      "%s: %.1f PSU\n%s",
      location,
      salinity,
      format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    pal = salinity_palette,
    values = salinity_data$salinity,
    title = "Salinity (PSU)",
    opacity = 0.9
  )
Salinity is shown in practical salinity units (PSU, parts per thousand). Lower values (blue) indicate fresher water; higher values (red) indicate more marine conditions.

title: NDBC Salinity Map
text:
Interpolated salinity field
The field below uses DIVAnd (Data-Interpolating Variational Analysis) with bathymetry masking, following the same approach as sfer-mbon-oxygen: ~0.01° horizontal grid resolution (~1 km), a two-scale analysis (smooth 0.08° background + 0.035° local anomalies with epsilon2 = 0.01), and interpolation run separately within each connected sea component so distant buoys do not smooth over local features. Land is masked using Natural Earth 10 m coastlines plus NOAA BlueTopo 4 m bathymetry (8 m tiles fill gaps); cells with missing bathymetry or elevation ≥ −0.2 m are treated as land. Sea cells are extended along water paths from each buoy (not across islands), then short bridges are added only through BlueTopo water cells (elevation < −0.2 m) to connect nearby stations still isolated from the main field.


Code
obs_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "salinity_obs.csv")
field_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "salinity_field.csv")
dir.create(dirname(obs_file), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

salinity_data |>
  select(longitude, latitude, salinity) |>
  write_csv(obs_file)

interp_log <- system2(
  "scripts/run_interpolate_field.sh",
  c(obs_file, field_file, "ndbc_buoys"),
  stdout = TRUE,
  stderr = TRUE
)
exit_code <- attr(interp_log, "status")
if (!is.null(exit_code) && exit_code != 0L) {
  stop(paste("Julia interpolation failed:\n", paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n")))
}
cat(paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n"), "\n")


Interpolating field from salinity_obs.csv
Loaded 19 observations for salinity
Grid size: 62 x 42 (2604 points)
Loaded BlueTopo (400x336 4 m) from ndbc_buoys_bluetopo_400x336.tif; land/unknown cells: 1014/2604 (NA and elev >= -0.2 m treated as land)
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.8013, 25.1672) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.4443, 25.2542) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.3964, 25.2893) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.7248, 25.1573) within 40 steps
Warning: could not bridge observation at (-80.3723, 25.2034) within 40 steps
Bridged 1 cell(s) along water paths for 1 observation(s)
Extended sea mask up to 4 step(s) along water from each observation
DIVAnd parameters: fine len_horiz=(0.035, 0.035), epsilon2=0.01; background len_horiz=(0.08, 0.08), epsilon2=1.0 (per sea component)
Observation fit at buoy grid cells: mean |residual|=0.02, max |residual|=0.08, RMSE=0.03
Kept 6 sea component(s) with observations; removed 0 disconnected cell(s)
Wrote data/interpolated/salinity_field.csv (1194 grid cells) 


Code
salinity_field <- read_csv(field_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
cat("Interpolated field cells:", nrow(salinity_field), "\n")


Interpolated field cells: 1194 


Code
source("R/plot_ndbc_field.R")




Code
if (nrow(salinity_field) == 0) {
  stop("Interpolated salinity field is empty.")
}

plot_salinity_field(salinity_field, salinity_data)






The colored grid shows the DIVAnd-interpolated field; circle markers show the buoy observations used as input.

Map extent: 24.3°N to 26.5°N, -83°W to -79.5°W.
Color scale: SST anomaly (sstAnom) using the dataset default BlueWhiteRed palette from −3°C to +3°C (blue = cooler than climatology, red = warmer).
Most recent analysis time in ERDDAP: 2026-07-12 UTC.

title: MUR SST Anomaly — Florida Keys
text:
Dataset details



Title
Multi-scale Ultra-high Resolution (MUR) SST Analysis Anomaly fv04.1, Global, 0.01°, 2002–present, Daily


Dataset ID
jplMURSST41anom1day


Institution
NOAA NMFS SWFSC ERD and NOAA NESDIS CoastWatch WCRN


Source product
JPL MUR L4 GHRSST v4.1 foundation SST


Variable
sstAnom — sea surface foundation temperature anomaly (°C)


Climatology
JPL daily MUR SST climatology, 2003–2014


Spatial resolution
0.01° (~1 km)


Temporal resolution
Daily (analysis time 09:00 UTC)


Coverage
Global, 2002-06-01 to present


Platforms / sensors
Aqua, Terra, Suomi-NPP, DMSP, NOAA-POES; AMSR-E, MODIS, VIIRS, AVHRR, SSM/I, in-situ


License
Free to use and redistribute; not intended for legal use (full disclaimer)




How the anomaly is computed
ERDDAP subtracts the JPL MUR 2003–2014 daily climatology from each day’s foundation SST analysis. For leap years, day-of-year 366 uses the day 365 climatology value. The most recent four days are typically revised each day; older days may also be updated.


ERDDAP image URL pattern
Each panel above is generated with a griddap PNG request of the form:
https://coastwatch.pfeg.noaa.gov/erddap/griddap/jplMURSST41anom1day.smallPng
  ?sstAnom[(TIME)][(24.3):(26.5)][(-83.0):(-79.5)]
  &.draw=surface
  &.vars=longitude|latitude|sstAnom
  &.colorBar=|||||
  &.land=over
Use the Make A Graph or Data Access Form pages to explore other extents, color bars, or file formats.


Related links

Dataset metadata
PO.DAAC background
ERDDAP griddap documentation

title: NDBC Temperature Map
text:
Code
library(dplyr)
library(httr2)
library(htmltools)
library(knitr)
library(leaflet)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(viridisLite)

# Station list and parameters mirror ndbc_fetch_airflow_dag.py
ndbc_stations <- list(
  list(name = "bkyf1", long_name = "Buoy_Key"),
  list(name = "bnkf1", long_name = "Butternut_Kay"),
  list(name = "bobf1", long_name = "Bob_Allen_Key"),
  list(name = "bwsf1", long_name = "Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "dkkf1", long_name = "Duck_Key"),
  list(name = "gbtf1", long_name = "Garfield_Bight"),
  list(name = "hcef1", long_name = "Highway_Creek"),
  list(name = "jbyf1", long_name = "Joe_Bay"),
  list(name = "jkyf1", long_name = "Johnson_Key"),
  list(name = "lbsf1", long_name = "Little_Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "lmdf1", long_name = "Little_Madeira_Bay"),
  list(name = "lrkf1", long_name = "Little_Rabbit_Key"),
  list(name = "lsnf1", long_name = "Long_Sound"),
  list(name = "mdkf1", long_name = "Middle_Key"),
  list(name = "mukf1", long_name = "Murray_Key"),
  list(name = "pkyf1", long_name = "Peterson_Key"),
  list(name = "tbyf1", long_name = "Terrapin_Bay"),
  list(name = "thrf1", long_name = "Thursday_Point"),
  list(name = "wrbf1", long_name = "Whipray_Basin")
)

# 25-hour lookback window
end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")
start_time <- format(Sys.time() - 25 * 3600, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")

fetch_latest_temperature <- function(station_info) {
  param <- "sea_water_temperature"
  url <- sprintf(
    "https://erddap.secoora.org/erddap/tabledap/gov-nps-ever-%s.csv?time%%2Clatitude%%2Clongitude%%2C%s&time%%3E=%s&time%%3C=%s",
    station_info$name,
    param,
    start_time,
    end_time
  )

  resp <- request(url) |>
    req_perform()

  df <- read_csv(resp_body_string(resp), show_col_types = FALSE)
  names(df) <- c("time", "latitude", "longitude", "temperature")
  df <- df[-1, ] |>
    mutate(
      time = as.POSIXct(time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC"),
      latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
      longitude = as.numeric(longitude),
      temperature = as.numeric(temperature)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(temperature))

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  latest <- df[which.max(df$time), ]
  tibble(
    station_id = station_info$name,
    station = station_info$long_name,
    location = sprintf(
      "%s (%s)",
      station_info$long_name,
      sub("f1$", "", station_info$name)
    ),
    time = latest$time,
    latitude = latest$latitude,
    longitude = latest$longitude,
    temperature = latest$temperature
  )
}

temperature_data <- bind_rows(lapply(ndbc_stations, fetch_latest_temperature))
report_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")

temperature_palette <- colorNumeric(
  palette = c("#313695", "#74add1", "#ffffbf", "#f46d43", "#a50026"),
  domain = temperature_data$temperature,
  na.color = "#999999"
)

leaflet(temperature_data) |>
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") |>
  setView(
    lng = mean(temperature_data$longitude),
    lat = mean(temperature_data$latitude),
    zoom = 9
  ) |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius = 8,
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = 1,
    color = "#333333",
    fillColor = ~temperature_palette(temperature),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    label = ~sprintf(
      "%s: %.1f °C\n%s",
      location,
      temperature,
      format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    pal = temperature_palette,
    values = temperature_data$temperature,
    title = "Temperature (°C)",
    opacity = 0.9
  )
Temperature is shown in degrees Celsius. Cooler values (blue) and warmer values (red) reflect spatial variation across the buoy network.

title: NDBC Temperature Map
text:
Interpolated temperature field
The field below uses DIVAnd (Data-Interpolating Variational Analysis) with bathymetry masking, following the same approach as sfer-mbon-oxygen: ~0.01° horizontal grid resolution (~1 km), a two-scale analysis (smooth 0.08° background + 0.035° local anomalies with epsilon2 = 0.01), and interpolation run separately within each connected sea component so distant buoys do not smooth over local features. Sea cells are extended along water paths from each buoy (not across islands), then short bridges are added only through BlueTopo water cells (elevation < −0.2 m) to connect nearby stations still isolated from the main field.

field_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "temperature_field.csv")
dir.create(dirname(obs_file), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

temperature_data |>
  select(longitude, latitude, temperature) |>
  write_csv(obs_file)

interp_log <- system2(
  "scripts/run_interpolate_field.sh",
  c(obs_file, field_file, "ndbc_buoys"),
  stdout = TRUE,
  stderr = TRUE
)
exit_code <- attr(interp_log, "status")
if (!is.null(exit_code) && exit_code != 0L) {
  stop(paste("Julia interpolation failed:\n", paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n")))
}
cat(paste(interp_log, collapse = "\n"), "\n")

}

  temperature_field,

  popup_suffix = " °C",
  palette = c("#313695", "#74add1", "#abd9e9", "#fee090", "#f46d43", "#a50026")
)

title: NDBC Salinity Time Series
text:
Code
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(htmltools)
library(httr2)

source("R/plot_grafana_salinity_ts.R")

ndbc_stations <- list(
  list(name = "bkyf1", long_name = "Buoy_Key"),
  list(name = "bnkf1", long_name = "Butternut_Kay"),
  list(name = "bobf1", long_name = "Bob_Allen_Key"),
  list(name = "bwsf1", long_name = "Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "dkkf1", long_name = "Duck_Key"),
  list(name = "gbtf1", long_name = "Garfield_Bight"),
  list(name = "hcef1", long_name = "Highway_Creek"),
  list(name = "jbyf1", long_name = "Joe_Bay"),
  list(name = "jkyf1", long_name = "Johnson_Key"),
  list(name = "lbsf1", long_name = "Little_Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "lmdf1", long_name = "Little_Madeira_Bay"),
  list(name = "lrkf1", long_name = "Little_Rabbit_Key"),
  list(name = "lsnf1", long_name = "Long_Sound"),
  list(name = "mdkf1", long_name = "Middle_Key"),
  list(name = "mukf1", long_name = "Murray_Key"),
  list(name = "pkyf1", long_name = "Peterson_Key"),
  list(name = "tbyf1", long_name = "Terrapin_Bay"),
  list(name = "thrf1", long_name = "Thursday_Point"),
  list(name = "wrbf1", long_name = "Whipray_Basin")
)

from_ms <- GRAFANA_DEFAULT_FROM
to_ms <- GRAFANA_DEFAULT_TO

salinity_ts <- fetch_grafana_salinity_timeseries(
  from = from_ms,
  to = to_ms
)

report_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")
range_start <- format(
  as.POSIXct(as.numeric(from_ms) / 1000, origin = "1970-01-01", tz = "UTC"),
  "%Y-%m-%d"
)
range_end <- format(
  as.POSIXct(as.numeric(to_ms) / 1000, origin = "1970-01-01", tz = "UTC"),
  "%Y-%m-%d"
)
Each panel below matches Grafana panel 33 (In-situ salinity (psu) at ~1m depth from $Buoy) on the USF MBON South Florida Salinity dashboard. Time series are queried from the dashboard’s InfluxDB datasource via the Grafana /api/ds/query endpoint, using the same query and time range as the dashboard link you provided.
Report generated: 2026-07-14 10:49 EDT
Time range: 2024-07-14 to 2026-07-14 UTC
Stations plotted: 19
Dashed lines mark the same low (25 PSU) and high (35 PSU) thresholds used on the Salinity Gauges page.

title: NDBC Salinity Gauges
text:
Code
library(dplyr)
library(httr2)
library(htmltools)
library(readr)
library(scales)

source("R/plot_salinity_gauges.R")

ndbc_stations <- list(
  list(name = "bkyf1", long_name = "Buoy_Key"),
  list(name = "bnkf1", long_name = "Butternut_Kay"),
  list(name = "bobf1", long_name = "Bob_Allen_Key"),
  list(name = "bwsf1", long_name = "Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "dkkf1", long_name = "Duck_Key"),
  list(name = "gbtf1", long_name = "Garfield_Bight"),
  list(name = "hcef1", long_name = "Highway_Creek"),
  list(name = "jbyf1", long_name = "Joe_Bay"),
  list(name = "jkyf1", long_name = "Johnson_Key"),
  list(name = "lbsf1", long_name = "Little_Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "lmdf1", long_name = "Little_Madeira_Bay"),
  list(name = "lrkf1", long_name = "Little_Rabbit_Key"),
  list(name = "lsnf1", long_name = "Long_Sound"),
  list(name = "mdkf1", long_name = "Middle_Key"),
  list(name = "mukf1", long_name = "Murray_Key"),
  list(name = "pkyf1", long_name = "Peterson_Key"),
  list(name = "tbyf1", long_name = "Terrapin_Bay"),
  list(name = "thrf1", long_name = "Thursday_Point"),
  list(name = "wrbf1", long_name = "Whipray_Basin")
)

end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")
start_time <- format(Sys.time() - 25 * 3600, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")

fetch_latest_salinity <- function(station_info) {
  param <- "sea_water_practical_salinity"
  url <- sprintf(
    "https://erddap.secoora.org/erddap/tabledap/gov-nps-ever-%s.csv?time%%2Clatitude%%2Clongitude%%2C%s&time%%3E=%s&time%%3C=%s",
    station_info$name,
    param,
    start_time,
    end_time
  )

  resp <- request(url) |>
    req_perform()

  df <- read_csv(resp_body_string(resp), show_col_types = FALSE)
  names(df) <- c("time", "latitude", "longitude", "salinity")
  df <- df[-1, ] |>
    mutate(
      time = as.POSIXct(time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC"),
      latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
      longitude = as.numeric(longitude),
      salinity = as.numeric(salinity)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(salinity))

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      station_id = station_info$name,
      station = station_info$long_name,
      location = sprintf(
        "%s (%s)",
        station_info$long_name,
        sub("f1$", "", station_info$name)
      ),
      time = as.POSIXct(NA, origin = "1970-01-01", tz = "UTC"),
      latitude = NA_real_,
      longitude = NA_real_,
      salinity = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  latest <- df[which.max(df$time), ]
  tibble(
    station_id = station_info$name,
    station = station_info$long_name,
    location = sprintf(
      "%s (%s)",
      station_info$long_name,
      sub("f1$", "", station_info$name)
    ),
    time = latest$time,
    latitude = latest$latitude,
    longitude = latest$longitude,
    salinity = latest$salinity
  )
}

salinity_data <- bind_rows(lapply(ndbc_stations, fetch_latest_salinity)) |>
  arrange(station)

report_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")
n_low <- sum(salinity_data$salinity < SALINITY_LOW_THRESHOLD, na.rm = TRUE)
n_high <- sum(salinity_data$salinity > SALINITY_HIGH_THRESHOLD, na.rm = TRUE)
n_normal <- sum(
  !is.na(salinity_data$salinity) &
    salinity_data$salinity >= SALINITY_LOW_THRESHOLD &
    salinity_data$salinity <= SALINITY_HIGH_THRESHOLD
)
n_missing <- sum(is.na(salinity_data$salinity))
Data are pulled from SECOORA ERDDAP using the same 25-hour window as the salinity map. Each gauge shows the latest non-missing reading for an Everglades National Park buoy.
Report generated: 2026-07-14 10:50 EDT
Query window: 2026-07-13T13:50:33Z to 2026-07-14T14:50:33Z

title: NDBC Salinity Gauges
text:
Thresholds
Gauges use practical salinity units (PSU) on a 10–50 scale suited to Florida Bay and the Keys:

Code
htmltools::tagList(salinity_gauge_legend())




Low: < 25 PSU



Normal: 25–35 PSU



High: > 35 PSU



Low (`< 25 PSU): fresher conditions, stronger freshwater or runoff influence
Normal (25–35 PSU): typical estuarine range for the region
High (`> 35 PSU): more marine or hypersaline conditions

Current summary: 1 low, 4 normal, 14 high, 0 missing.
